evals.name: completeness-all-sections
evals.prompt: Create a product brief for: A mobile app that uses AI to match dog owners with compatible dog walkers based on dog temperament, owner preferences, and walker experience.
evals.expected_output: Complete brief with all sections, all three output formats
assertions: HTML output exists in /mnt/user-data/outputs/
DOCX output exists in /mnt/user-data/outputs/
Markdown output exists in /mnt/user-data/outputs/
output contains Decision Dashboard with color-coded metrics
output contains Problem section with quantified market pain
output contains Solution section in present tense with user scenario
output contains 'What This Is NOT' scope exclusions
output contains Why Now section with specific timing catalyst
output contains How It Works section
output contains Market section with TAM and competitive table
output contains Business Model section with pricing
output contains Risks section with confidence tags per risk
output contains scenario branching with at least 2 What If paths
output contains PR/FAQ section with press release + external + internal FAQs
output contains Expert Panel with 4 expert responses in Yes/And format
output contains convergent additions in expert synthesis
output contains living document markers with freshness dates
no marketing superlatives (revolutionary, game-changing, disruptive)
no framework-specific jargon (no crossbar, void, vertex, navigation, toroid)
The Ask section is present and shorter than all other sections

evals.name: prior-analysis-integration
evals.prompt: Create a product brief for a privacy-first legal research tool for everyday Americans. Use analysis_id: 391. The prior analysis found that the key strategic insight is the difference between deletion architecture and absorption architecture, identified adversarial exploitation as a blind spot, and surfaced comprehension-in-safety as the product's experiential shape.
evals.expected_output: Brief that integrates prior analysis findings in product language
assertions: output contains Key Insights section
output references absorption vs deletion as strategic advantage — not as framework term
output references adversarial exploitation as market risk — not as framework term
output uses product briefing language throughout
no instances of: crossbar, void, vertex, surviving vertices, toroid, navigation anchor
Risks section addresses unauthorized practice of law concerns
Why Now section references a specific timing catalyst
expert panel experts use domain-specific perspective for legal-tech

evals.name: citation-integrity
evals.prompt: Create a product brief for: A SaaS platform that helps small restaurants optimize their food waste using computer vision on existing security cameras.
evals.expected_output: Brief where every claim is sourced
assertions: every market size claim has source attribution
every competitor mention has source attribution
every regulatory reference has source attribution
no fabricated statistics
unknowns explicitly tagged UNKNOWN
HTML has hover tooltips on sourced claims
DOCX has footnotes for sourced claims
Markdown has inline [Source:] citations
confidence tags appear on individual claims not just sections

evals.name: expert-panel-quality
evals.prompt: Create a product brief for: An AI-powered tool that automatically generates legally binding contracts from natural language descriptions, targeting freelancers. Domain: legal-tech.
assertions: Director of Product thinks as legal-tech product leader — not generic
CEO thinks as legal-tech CEO — not generic startup CEO
Sr Principal Engineer raises legal validity or compliance concern
GTM Specialist identifies specific acquisition channel for freelancers
all experts use Yes/And format — not pure criticism
at least one expert verdict is ITERATE or PASS
synthesis identifies convergent additions from 2+ experts
expert responses are included in full in all three output formats

evals.name: confidence-and-scenarios
evals.prompt: Create a product brief for: A browser extension that detects dark patterns on e-commerce sites and alerts users before checkout.
evals.expected_output: Brief with confidence system and scenario branching
assertions: confidence tags appear on individual claims not just sections
at least 3 different confidence levels used across the brief
confidence tags are color-coded in HTML output
Risks section contains at least 2 'What If' scenarios
each What If scenario has: trigger condition, impact, and response
living document markers present with freshness dates
Decision Dashboard at top has color-coded metrics
at least one claim tagged UNKNOWN or SPECULATIVE (honest about gaps)
